text.more: The War on Drugs officially began in 1971, when President Richard Nixon wrote a special message to Congress declaring the problem of drug abuse to have reached the level of a national emergency. Calling for a coordinated federal response, he cited statistics on drug-related deaths and linked the fight against drugs to that of a moral imperative (Peters). Since then, a number of conspiracy theories have cropped up with alternative views on the true reasoning behind initiating the War on Drugs: one being that the War on Drugs does not exist and is actually a government scam to offset the national debt. This network visualizes the actors associated with this conspiracy theory and their relationships and affiliations.
text.intro: Social affiliation network of people and groups named as evidence by David J. Stewart in his conspiracy theory.
text.title: The War on Drugs as a Scam
